input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
                <!-- wrapping trick isn't needed; see below -->
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>
</ContentPage>
```

Actually, the idiomatic approach is to **wrap** the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel** (using MVVM Community Toolkit):

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api, INavigationService nav)
    {
        _api = api;
        _nav = nav;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;          // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;           // reset so re-selection works
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Drives the list |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | — | Enables single-select visuals |
| `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | `SelectedItem` | Tracks which item is tapped |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires on tap → navigates |
| `IsRefreshing` (on RefreshView) | `IsRefreshing` | Controls the spinner |
| `Command` (on RefreshView) | `RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull → reloads data |

**Two things to remember:**
1. **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigation — otherwise tapping the same item again won't fire the command.
2. **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** at the end of your refresh logic — the spinner won't stop on its own.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about .NET MAUI CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh, plus navigation on selection and data reloading. This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/XAML development - I can answer directly without needing tools.
